thinking.text:
The user has tried multiple times to verify their identity but hasn't been able to provide a second piece of information that matches what we have on file. They've asked if there's another way to verify or if they can speak with a human agent.

According to the policy:
- I should not transfer to a human agent without asking the user first
- The user has now asked to speak with a human agent
- I've exhausted the verification options available to me

The user has explicitly asked to speak with a human agent, and I cannot verify their identity through the available methods. This seems like an appropriate situation to transfer to a human agent who may have additional verification methods or can help resolve this issue.

Let me transfer them to a human agent with a summary of what happened.

tool_calls.Name: transfer_to_human_agents
tool_calls.Arguments: {"reason": "customer_requests_human_no_specific_reason", "summary": "Customer Fatima Al-Hassan is trying to access the Business Gold Rewards Card referral program. Phone number (313-555-0246) was verified, but customer was unable to provide a matching second piece of verification information (tried DOB March 18, 1993, email fatima.coffee@gmail.com, and address 1427 West Grand Blvd, Detroit, MI 48208 — none matched records). Customer has requested human assistance to complete verification and access the referral link tool."}

worker_attempts.visible_answer:
I completely understand — verification can sometimes be tricky, especially if there's been a mix-up with the information on file.

Since we haven't been able to verify your identity through the standard methods, I'd be happy to transfer you to a human agent who can help. They may have additional ways to verify your identity or can assist with updating your account information if needed.
